mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, tight labor market conditions force wage growth that boosts consumer spending power, directly lifting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in housing starts and industrial production indicate synchronized real-economy expansion across residential and commercial sectors, which scales aggregate corporate revenue and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero directional accuracy below 50 percent over any 3-year rolling window, indicating the proxies do not jointly predict equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, a rising CPIAUCSL index outpacing the FEDFUNDS rate compresses real yields and signals deteriorating purchasing power, which forces equity risk premium expansion and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite rising real yield compression, falsifying the valuation channel.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly scales forward earnings expectations for S&P 500 constituents, triggering immediate positive price discovery and generating positive forward returns over a 5-day trading horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no statistically significant correlation with quarterly GDP growth surprises (p > 0.10) across a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index exceeding 1.0 percent signals strong manufacturing demand and capacity utilization, which lifts cyclical sector earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 150 basis points over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales confirms that employment gains translate directly into consumer spending, sustaining corporate revenue momentum and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Partial correlation analysis shows PAYEMS and RSAFS do not jointly explain more than 5 percent of variance in 60-day equity returns after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, persistent CPIAUCSL readings above 2.5 percent force market pricing of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses forward valuation multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand despite CPIAUCSL remaining above 2.5 percent for three consecutive months, indicating the market prices inflation as transitory rather than policy-driven.
